0019:507A.label: func_test_req_flag
0019:507A.comment:
Tests bit 7 of request-record byte at [ES:DI+0x06] and returns with ZF
reflecting the result (ZF=1 if bit is clear). Two-instruction helper; called
immediately after func_init_transfer_state, with result checked by caller's je/jne.

0019:71D5.label: func_save_req_ptr
0019:71D5.comment:
Sets the 'request in progress' flag ([0x0357]=1) and snapshots the current
request-header far pointer ([0x059e]=offset, [0x05a0]=segment) into the
saved-pointer slot ([0x05e6:05e8]). Counterpart func_0019_71CF clears the flag.

0019:5453.label: func_write_char_int29h
0019:5453.comment: Write character in AL via INT 29h (fast console output) if the current output device is a character device that supports it. Calls func_0019_756F to locate the active output device record via SI. Checks device attribute bits and the DOS CON driver header for INT 29h support before issuing the interrupt. Returns with CF clear on success.

0019:54D7.label: func_console_output_char
0019:54D7.comment: Write character in AL to the console with column tracking and periodic housekeeping. Printable chars (0x20-0x7E) increment the column counter [0x01F9]; DEL (0x7F) outputs without incrementing. CR resets the column counter to 0 before output. BS and TAB have handlers in the gap (not observed in this log). Every 64 characters the throttle counter [0x0300] wraps to 0 and func_0019_90DB is called for periodic work. Output goes via func_write_char_int29h. If redirect flag [0x02FE] is non-zero after output, an alternate path (not observed) is taken.

0019:76F7.label: func_init_transfer_state
0019:76F7.comment:
Initializes driver transfer-state variables from the current request record (ES:DI).
Loads the buffer offset from [di+0x07] via lds (SI=0x0023); discards the lds-loaded
segment and hardcodes 0x0070 as the output-buffer segment, saving the far ptr to
[0x058a:058c]. Saves the current output cursor [0x032c] to [0x05b8]. Clears byte
counter [0x0574]. Snapshots the 32-bit start position [di+0x15:17] to [0x05ce:05d0].
Tests request flags [di+0x05] for bits 0x8080; returns AX = CX + [0x032c] (total
items to transfer). The caller uses AX as the new CX for func_0019_78CF, which
advances [di+0x15:17] by that count.

0019:4256.label: func_save_ctx
0019:4256.comment:
Saves the current register context for later restoration by func_restore_ctx
(0019:423F). Pops its own return address into [0x05ee], pushes DS/BP/DI/SI/DX/CX/BX/AX
onto the stack, then pushes the saved return address back. Also restores ES from the
value pushed by the caller immediately before `call 0x4256`. Returns normally; the
stacked registers are later unwound by func_restore_ctx, which jumps to [0x05ee].
Called at the start of func_issue_cmd (0019:83D8) to snapshot incoming registers
before invoking the CD-ROM driver.

0019:423F.label: func_restore_ctx
0019:423F.comment:
Restores the register context saved by func_save_ctx (0019:4256). Pops the caller's
return address into [0x05ee], pops AX/BX/CX/DX/SI/DI/BP/DS from the saved frame,
then jumps to [0x05ee]. Flags at the jump reflect whatever was set before the
`call 0x423f` (typically `and ah, 0x02` in func_issue_cmd), so callers see the
driver success/failure flag after restoration.

0019:5036.label: func_dispatch_error
0019:5036.comment:
Dispatches on a command/error code by patching the caller's saved return address.
Stack on entry: [..., return-to-caller, table-ptr, cmd-code, ...]. Reads table header
byte CS:[table_ptr] for max entries; if cmd < max, computes handler address from
CS:[table_ptr + 1 + cmd*2] and overwrites the saved return address ([bp+0x06]) with
it. After stack cleanup, `ret` 'returns' to the handler instead of the normal
continuation. For command AL=0x06 (Read Long), table[6] = 0019:92E7, which fires
INT 24h (CDR101: Not ready). Called once from lbl_0019_46E3 with AX=0x1206
(function 0x12, error code 0x06) and dispatch table at CS:0x3F6C.

0019:51A6.label: func_wait_for_data
0019:51A6.comment:
Polling loop: waits for the CD-ROM drive to become ready with data. Calls
func_poll_drive (0019:90DB) in a loop; on success (ZF=1) sends Read Status
(AH=0x05) via func_issue_cmd, fires INT 2Ah (multiplex yield), increments retry
counter [0x0d91], and loops. On failure (ZF=0) calls func_issue_cmd AH=0x00
(abort), restores registers, and returns with error in AL. In this trace the drive
never became ready after 82 polls; loop exited with AL=0x0D.

0019:756F.label: func_get_device_record
0019:756F.comment:
Returns DS:SI pointing to the MSCDEX device record for drive BX. If [0x0357]
(request in-progress flag) is 0, calls func_0019_A62A to look up the record and
sets DS=0x0116, SI=record offset. If [0x0357]=1 and BX<=1, loads the saved request
far pointer directly from [0x05e6:05e8]. Returns CF clear on success. Hot path:
called 147 times (82 times from func_poll_drive, 62 from func_write_char_int29h,
1 from func_console_output_char, 1 from func_0019_55CD, 1 from func_0019_90DB).

0019:83D8.label: func_issue_cmd
0019:83D8.comment:
Issues a CD-ROM device driver command and returns the result. AH = command code
(0x00=abort, 0x01=Media Check, 0x04=..., 0x05=Read Status, 0x84=...). Saves
context via func_save_ctx (0019:4256), sets up the request block far pointer
([0x038a:038c] = 0x0116:0x03bc), translates AH via dispatch table at CS:0x83C6/
0x83CC, then invokes the driver via func_load_driver_ptr/func_call_cdrom_driver
(0019:85AF/85B2). Returns AX=[0x03bc] (driver result) with flags set by
`not [0x0380]; and 0x02`: ZF=1 on success (bit 1 of [0x0380] is set by SBPCD.SYS
during its interrupt routine). ZF=0 indicates driver failure ('not ready');
the one failing call (84th, AH=0x01 from func_poll_drive) returns AL=0x0D.

0019:85AF.label: func_load_driver_ptr
0019:85AF.comment:
Loads the CD-ROM driver data pointer from the MSCDEX device record, then falls
through to func_call_cdrom_driver (0019:85B2). Reads the far pointer at [si+7]
(stored in the device record at 0x0116:0x0114) → DS:SI = 0x0070:0x0023 (SBPCD.SYS
request area). Called from func_issue_cmd (0019:83D8) 167 times.

0019:85B2.label: func_call_cdrom_driver
0019:85B2.comment:
Invokes the CD-ROM device driver strategy and interrupt routines. Checks device
attribute bit 7 at [si+0x05] (0x0070:0x0028 = 0x80, always set for CD-ROM), then
reads far addresses of strategy ([si+6]=0x06F5) and interrupt ([si+8]=0x0700)
routines. Calls strategy then interrupt via far ptr at [0x0378:037a] (segment
0x0070). Strategy stores request pointer; interrupt executes the command and fills
the request block including the [0x0380] status byte read by func_issue_cmd.
Called 250 times (167 via func_load_driver_ptr + 83 direct from func_read_status).
After each call, func_0019_876A checks [0x0db7] (always 0x00, never retries).

0019:9024.label: func_read_status
0019:9024.comment:
Sends Read Status (command 0x05) to the CD-ROM driver and tests for success.
Sets up the request: command=0x05 at [0x0394], length=0x0E at [0x0392], clears
[0x0395:0396]. Loads the SBPCD.SYS request area via DS:SI=[0x0032] = 0x0070:0x0023
and calls func_call_cdrom_driver (0019:85B2). After return, tests [0x0396] bit 1
for 'drive ready'; in this trace always succeeds ([0x0396]=0x03, bit 1 set),
returns AL=0. Called 83 times from func_poll_drive before each Media Check.

0019:90DB.label: func_poll_drive
0019:90DB.comment:
Polls the CD-ROM drive once. First calls func_read_status (0019:9024) to verify
drive state, then calls func_get_device_record (0019:756F, BX=0) to get DS:SI.
Issues Media Check (AH=0x01) via func_issue_cmd (0019:83D8). On success (ZF=1)
goes to lbl_0019_90AD which yields via INT 28h and returns ZF=1 to the caller.
On failure, filters error code: AL=0x13→long timeout path (gap), AL=0x10→retry
gap, AL=0x03→soft return. Any other code (including the observed AL=0x0D) falls
through to a bare `ret` at 0019:90A9, propagating ZF=0 and AL to the caller.
The 84th call (from func_wait_for_data loop) returns AL=0x0D, ZF=0, triggering
the 'not ready' error.

0019:A60D.label: func_get_drive_entry
0019:A60D.comment:
Sets DS=0x0116 from [0x3dd7]/[0x0330], bounds-checks BX against drive count
at [0x0032], then sets ES:DI = base of drive array [0x0034] + BX. Returns ES:DI
pointing to the BX-th logical drive mapping entry, CF set if out of range.
Called from func_lookup_drive_entry (0019:A62A) and func_0019_A7DD.

0019:A62A.label: func_lookup_drive_entry
0019:A62A.comment:
Looks up the MSCDEX device record for logical drive BX. Calls func_get_drive_entry
(0019:A60D) to locate the drive entry in the mapping table, checks [di]=0xFF
(invalid/unassigned). Reads the physical drive number from [di] into BL and
calls func_calc_device_record (0019:A644) to get ES:DI pointing to the record.

0019:A644.label: func_calc_device_record
0019:A644.comment:
Computes ES:DI pointing to the MSCDEX device record for physical drive BX.
Loads the device table at ES:DI from [0x002a] = 0x0116:0x00CC; bounds-checks
BX against entry count at [di+4] (=5). Record stride is 0x3B bytes; DI =
0x00CC + 0x3B*BX + 6. For BX=1 (observed): DI = 0x010D.

0C45:218E.label: func_sbpcd_get_attn_status
0C45:218E.comment:
Sends hardware cmd 0x81 (Get Attention/Drive Status) and reads the 1-byte result.
Writes 0x81 to base+0, then polls base+1 bit 2 for busy=0 (up to 5×65536 loops).
On timeout returns CF=1. On success reads base+0 → AL, stores AL to [CS:0x0053],
returns CF=0. The result byte encodes drive state:
  bit 3 (0x08) = drive ready / data disc present
  bit 4 (0x10) = audio playing
  bit 6 (0x40) = disc stable / not changed (bit clear = media changed)
  bit 0 (0x01) = status-changed / ATN event (triggers geometry re-init at 0x0D33)
Called from the ATN handler at 0x0C56 after base+1 returned ATN=0x01.

0C45:1AAE.label: func_sbpcd_read_disc_pos
0C45:1AAE.comment:
Sends hardware cmd 0x8B (Read Disc Position/Mode, 6 result bytes) via
func_sbpcd_issue_cmd_read (0x1FB7). On success processes the 6-byte result
buffer at [CS:0x9D]:
  byte 0 → [CS:0x0075] (mode byte)
  bytes 1-2 → [CS:0x003F/0x0040]
  bytes 3-5 → [CS:0x0041] (AX), [CS:0x0043] (DX)
Then executes `or byte [CS:0x2E], 0x10` to set bit 4 of the drive-init flags,
returns CF=0. On command failure returns CF=1 (AL=0xFF).
Called from 0x0CFA when [CS:0x002E] bit 4 is clear and [CS:0x0053] bit 0 is set.

0C45:1FB7.label: func_sbpcd_issue_cmd_read
0C45:1FB7.comment:
Top-level helper: sets up a result buffer pointer, sends the command bytes in
[CS:0x0082]/[CS:0x0083] to the drive, then reads CX result bytes from base+0
into the buffer via func_sbpcd_read_result_loop (0x227D). The command byte
counts and buffer address are pre-configured by the caller in [CS:0x0082] and CX.
Returns CF=0 on success, CF=1 on timeout.

0C45:227D.label: func_sbpcd_read_result_loop
0C45:227D.comment:
Reads CX result bytes from base+0 into ES:DI buffer. For each byte, polls
base+1 bit 2 for not-busy (up to 0x8000 loops); on timeout sets CF=1 and returns
early. On not-busy reads base+0 via `in al, dx` and stores with `stosb`.
Special case: if [CS:0x0082]=0x84 or CX=0, returns immediately CF=0 (no data).

lineComments.0019:507A 26 F6 45 06 80: test bit 7 of request-record byte at offset 0x06; ZF=1 if clear
lineComments.0019:71DB A1 9E 05: load offset of current request-header far ptr
lineComments.0019:71E1 A1 A0 05: load segment of current request-header far ptr
lineComments.0019:76FB 36 8C 1E 8C 05: hardcode 0x0070 as output-buffer segment (lds-loaded segment is discarded)
lineComments.0019:7722 26 F7 45 05 80 80: test error/type bits 7 and 15 of request flags
lineComments.0019:7768 03 06 2C 03: AX = CX + prior output cursor → total items to transfer, returned to caller
lineComments.0019:5050 2E 8B 1F: load handler address from dispatch table: CS:[table_base + 1 + cmd*2]
lineComments.0019:5053 89 5E 06: overwrite saved return address in caller's frame → 'ret' jumps to error handler
lineComments.0019:51AB 75 3B: if func_poll_drive returned ZF=0 (driver error), exit polling loop with error in AL
lineComments.0019:8424 2E 8A A7 C6 83: look up command-type byte from table CS:0x83C6[AH]
lineComments.0019:8458 8A 26 80 03: driver response status: SBPCD.SYS writes this; bit 1 set = success, clear = not ready
lineComments.0019:845E 80 E4 02: ZF=1 (success) if bit 1 was set; ZF=0 → 'not ready', AL will be [0x03bc] error code
lineComments.0019:8614 36 FF 1E 78 03: call CD-ROM driver strategy routine (0x0070:0x06F5) — stores request block pointer
lineComments.0019:8620 36 FF 1E 78 03: call CD-ROM driver interrupt routine (0x0070:0x0700) — executes command, fills request block
lineComments.0019:9056 36 F6 06 96 03 02: bit 1 of Read Status result byte [0x0396]: 1 = drive ready (always set in this trace)
lineComments.0019:90EC 74 BF: success path (ZF=1, taken 83 times); once ZF=0 with AL=0x0D triggers 'not ready' exit
lineComments.0019:9337 CD 24: DOS Critical Error Handler: produces 'CDR101: Not ready reading drive D'
lineComments.0019:A666 F6 E3: BX×0x3B: offset into device record table (0x3B bytes per record)
lineComments.0C45:0C52 A8 01: ATN check: bit 0 of base+1 result; ZF=1 → no ATN → je 0x0C6D taken; ZF=0 → ATN → fall to 0x0C56 ATN handler (call func_sbpcd_get_attn_status, dispatch on bits 6/4 of result)
lineComments.0C45:0C54 74 17: skip ATN handler (0x0C56-0x0C6C): calls func_sbpcd_get_attn_status, re-reads [0x0053]; result bit 6=1→bit 4 check; bit 4=1→audio path (0x0C8A); bit 4=0→fall to 0x0C6D
lineComments.0C45:0D23 2E F6 06 2E 00 04: test drive-init flag [0x002e] bit 2 (set by 0x0D94 after cmd 0x88 geometry setup); if set → dispatch command; if clear → check [0x0053] bit 0
lineComments.0C45:0D29 75 6F: if [0x002e] bit 2 already set (drive previously initialized) → dispatch command directly
lineComments.0C45:0D2B 2E F6 06 53 00 01: fallback: if [0x0053] (cmd 0x81 result) bit 0 is CLEAR → also dispatch without re-init; bit 0 SET → fall to 0x0D33 geometry init path
lineComments.0C45:0D31 74 67: dispatch without geometry init (bit 0 clear = no disc-change event); bit 0 set triggers 0x0D33: cmd 0x88 → `or [0x002e],0x04` at 0x0D94
lineComments.0C45:199F 2E F6 06 2E 00 04: SECOND [0x002e] bit-2 check inside command handler: if clear (drive never initialized) → je 0x19BF → jmp 0x0DAE → error 0x81/0x02 (not ready); this is the actual failure point in the current trace
lineComments.0C45:19A5 74 18: if [0x002e] bit 2 not set → skip secondary checks → 0x19BF → not-ready error path
lineComments.0C45:19BF E9 EC F3: not-ready branch: → 0x0DAE (mov al,0x02; mov ah,0x81) → writes error status to request block → MSCDEX sees failure at 0019:8458
